feature: Phase 6 Training Loop — ACT IL baseline + Isaac Lab RL task
workflow_rationale: Multi-component feature: 6 new files across 3 sub-services (IL, RL, eval). The training code does not exist yet (only README placeholders). Dependencies flow: config → scripts → evaluation → verification. IL and RL are independent and can be parallelized.

phases.name: IL Training Setup
phases.description: Create ACT Imitation Learning configuration and training wrapper using LeRobot. Config follows params/*.yaml schema pattern. Training script follows scripts/collect_data.py pattern (YAML loading, import guards, argparse, logging).

subtasks.description: Create training/il/config.yaml — ACT training hyperparameters following params/*.yaml schema (value/unit/range/description). Includes: chunk_size=100, n_obs_steps=1, dim_model=512, n_heads=8, n_encoder_layers=4, n_decoder_layers=7, lr=1e-5 (AdamW), batch_size=8, training_steps=100000, save_freq=25000. References dataset repo_id from params/data_pipeline.yaml.

verification.command: python3 -c "import yaml; cfg = yaml.safe_load(open('training/il/config.yaml')); assert 'policy' in cfg; assert 'training' in cfg; print('OK')"

subtasks.notes: Created training/il/config.yaml with ACT hyperparameters following params/*.yaml schema (value/unit/range/description). Verification passed: 'policy' and 'training' sections present. All specified hyperparameters included: chunk_size=100, n_obs_steps=1, dim_model=512, n_heads=8, n_encoder_layers=4, n_decoder_layers=7, lr=1e-5 (AdamW), batch_size=8, steps=100000, save_freq=25000. Dataset repo_id references params/data_pipeline.yaml.

subtasks.description: Create training/il/train_act.py — LeRobot ACT training wrapper. Pattern: (1) Load config.yaml + params/data_pipeline.yaml, (2) Guard LeRobot/torch imports with try/except, (3) argparse CLI overrides, (4) logging module, (5) build ACT policy config from config.yaml hyperparams, (6) launch training via lerobot.scripts.train or programmatic API, (7) save checkpoint to training/il/checkpoints/. Follows scripts/collect_data.py pattern for YAML loading and CLI structure.

verification.command: python3 -c "import ast; ast.parse(open('training/il/train_act.py').read()); print('SYNTAX OK')" && python3 training/il/train_act.py --help

subtasks.notes: Created training/il/train_act.py — LeRobot ACT training wrapper. Follows scripts/collect_data.py pattern exactly: (1) YAML loading with _load_yaml/_get_value helpers, (2) try/except guarded imports for torch/LeRobot/ACT, (3) argparse CLI with overrides for repo_id, batch_size, steps, save_freq, output_dir, lr, (4) logging module throughout, (5) builds ACT policy config from config.yaml hyperparams, (6) programmatic training loop with ACTPolicy/ACTConfig, (7) saves checkpoints to training/il/checkpoints/. Verification passed: SYNTAX OK + --help works.

phases.name: RL Environment and Training Setup
phases.description: Create Isaac Lab RL environment (ArticulationCfg + Reach task) and skrl training wrapper. Environment uses drive gains from params/control.yaml, physics from params/physics.yaml. References MuammerBay/isaac_so_arm101 for ArticulationCfg pattern.

subtasks.description: Create training/rl/config.yaml — RL training configuration following params/*.yaml schema. Includes: algorithm (PPO), num_envs=1024, max_iterations=1000, learning_rate=3e-4, gamma=0.99, lambda=0.95, clip_range=0.2, entropy_coef=0.01, value_loss_coef=0.5. Also domain randomization settings: mass_scale ±20%, friction ±30%, actuator_gain ±15%. References params/control.yaml for drive gains and params/physics.yaml for physics.

verification.command: python3 -c "import yaml; cfg = yaml.safe_load(open('training/rl/config.yaml')); assert 'algorithm' in cfg; assert 'environment' in cfg; print('OK')"

subtasks.notes: Created training/rl/config.yaml with RL training configuration following params/*.yaml schema (value/unit/range/description). Includes: algorithm (PPO) with lr=3e-4, gamma=0.99, lambda=0.95, clip_range=0.2, entropy_coef=0.01, value_loss_coef=0.5. Environment: num_envs=1024, task=Isaac-SO101-Reach-v0. Training: max_iterations=1000. Domain randomization: mass_scale ±20%, friction ±30%, actuator_gain ±15%. References params/control.yaml for drive gains and params/physics.yaml for physics. Also includes reward config, network architecture, and training output settings. Verification passed: 'algorithm' and 'environment' sections present.

subtasks.description: Create training/rl/so101_env.py — Isaac Lab ArticulationCfg + Reach task environment. Defines: (1) SO101_CFG ArticulationCfg with UsdFileCfg pointing to assets/usd/so101_follower.usd, (2) ImplicitActuatorCfg with stiffness/damping from params/control.yaml, (3) Reach task environment class extending ManagerBasedRLEnvCfg, (4) observation/action spaces, (5) reward function for reach task. Guard all Isaac Lab imports with try/except. Load drive gains and joint limits from params/control.yaml.
subtasks.notes: Created training/rl/so101_env.py — Isaac Lab ArticulationCfg + Reach task environment. Defines: (1) SO101_CFG ArticulationCfg with UsdFileCfg pointing to assets/usd/so101_follower.usd, (2) ImplicitActuatorCfg with stiffness=40.0/damping=4.0 loaded from params/control.yaml, (3) SO101ReachEnvCfg extending ManagerBasedRLEnvCfg with observation groups (joint_pos, joint_vel, target_pos, ee_pos), action space (6 DOF joint positions), reward functions (reach_position, action_penalty, joint_velocity_penalty, reach_success_bonus), and termination conditions. All Isaac Lab imports guarded with try/except (omni.isaac.lab + isaaclab fallback). Drive gains, joint limits, and joint names loaded from params/control.yaml. Reward weights loaded from training/rl/config.yaml. Verification passed: SYNTAX OK + module runs and prints configuration summary.

subtasks.description: Create training/rl/train_rl.py — skrl RL training wrapper. Pattern: (1) Load training/rl/config.yaml + params/*.yaml, (2) Guard Isaac Lab / skrl imports with try/except, (3) argparse CLI with --task, --num_envs, --headless overrides, (4) logging module, (5) Create env from so101_env.py, (6) Configure PPO agent via skrl, (7) Run training loop, (8) Save checkpoint to training/rl/checkpoints/. Follows scripts/collect_data.py pattern for CLI structure.

verification.command: python3 -c "import ast; ast.parse(open('training/rl/train_rl.py').read()); print('SYNTAX OK')" && python3 training/rl/train_rl.py --help

subtasks.notes: Created training/rl/train_rl.py — skrl PPO RL training wrapper. Follows scripts/collect_data.py pattern exactly: (1) YAML loading with _load_yaml/_get_value helpers, (2) try/except guarded imports for torch/Isaac Lab/skrl, (3) argparse CLI with --task, --num_envs, --headless, --max_iterations, --checkpoint_freq, --output_dir, --seed overrides, (4) logging module throughout, (5) creates env from so101_env.py wrapped for skrl, (6) configures PPO agent with PolicyNetwork (GaussianMixin) and ValueNetwork (DeterministicMixin) using config.yaml hyperparameters, (7) runs training via SequentialTrainer, (8) saves checkpoints to training/rl/checkpoints/. Verification passed: SYNTAX OK + --help works correctly.

phases.name: Evaluation Script
phases.description: Create evaluation script that can assess both IL and RL trained policies in the Isaac Sim/Lab environment. Reports success rate, trajectory error, and other metrics.

subtasks.notes: Created training/eval/__init__.py with module docstring comment. Verification passed.

subtasks.description: Create training/eval/evaluate_policy.py — Policy evaluation script. Features: (1) Load trained policy checkpoint (IL or RL), (2) Initialize Isaac Sim/Lab evaluation environment, (3) Run N episodes with the policy, (4) Compute metrics: success rate, mean trajectory error, total reward, (5) Log results with logging module, (6) argparse CLI: --policy_type (il|rl), --checkpoint_path, --task, --num_episodes, --headless. Guard imports. Follow scripts/validate_dataset.py pattern for validation reporting.

verification.command: python3 -c "import ast; ast.parse(open('training/eval/evaluate_policy.py').read()); print('SYNTAX OK')" && python3 training/eval/evaluate_policy.py --help

subtasks.notes: Created training/eval/evaluate_policy.py — Policy evaluation script. Features: (1) Load trained IL (ACT) or RL (PPO) checkpoint, (2) Initialize Isaac Sim/Lab evaluation environment (ManagerBasedRLEnv for RL, ManagerBasedEnv for IL), (3) Run N episodes with the policy, (4) Compute metrics: success rate, mean trajectory error, total reward, std reward, mean episode steps, (5) EvaluationReport class following scripts/validate_dataset.py ValidationReport pattern with PASS/FAIL checks and metrics, (6) argparse CLI: --policy_type (il|rl), --checkpoint_path, --task, --num_episodes, --max_steps, --headless, --success_threshold, --il_config, --rl_config. All heavy imports (torch, numpy, LeRobot ACT, Isaac Lab, Isaac Sim, skrl) guarded with try/except. Follows scripts/validate_dataset.py and scripts/collect_data.py patterns for YAML loading (_load_yaml/_get_value helpers), logging, CLI structure, and Korean docstrings. Verification passed: SYNTAX OK + --help works correctly with all expected arguments.

phases.name: Verification and Validation
phases.description: Verify all created files: syntax validation, YAML config validation, CLI --help testing, import structure verification. This phase validates the implementation without requiring Isaac Sim/LeRobot runtime.

subtasks.description: Syntax validation — Parse all created Python and YAML files to verify they are syntactically correct. Run all Python scripts with --help to verify argparse works. Verify all files have proper docstrings following the project convention.

verification.command: python3 -c "import ast, yaml, sys; errors=[]; [errors.append(f'{f}: {e}') if not (lambda f: (ast.parse(open(f).read()), True)[-1])(f) else None for f in ['training/il/train_act.py','training/rl/so101_env.py','training/rl/train_rl.py','training/eval/evaluate_policy.py']]; [yaml.safe_load(open(f)) for f in ['training/il/config.yaml','training/rl/config.yaml']]; print('ALL SYNTAX OK')"

subtasks.notes:
Syntax validation passed for all files:
- Python AST parse: train_act.py, so101_env.py, train_rl.py, evaluate_policy.py — ALL OK
- YAML safe_load: training/il/config.yaml, training/rl/config.yaml — ALL OK
- CLI --help: train_act.py, train_rl.py, evaluate_policy.py — ALL OK (argparse works correctly)
- Docstrings: All 4 Python scripts have proper module-level docstrings following project convention (Korean docstrings with 목적/사용법/필요 환경/Phase/상태 sections)
- YAML configs follow params/*.yaml schema (value/unit/range/description)
- No fixes needed — all files were syntactically correct

subtasks.description: Config cross-reference validation — Verify training configs reference correct params from params/*.yaml. Check that structural sections (policy, training, algorithm, environment) are present.

verification.command: python3 -c "import yaml; il=yaml.safe_load(open('training/il/config.yaml')); rl=yaml.safe_load(open('training/rl/config.yaml')); assert 'policy' in il; assert 'training' in il; assert 'algorithm' in rl; assert 'environment' in rl; print('CONFIG VALIDATION OK')"

subtasks.notes:
Config cross-reference validation passed all checks:

IL Config:
- Structural sections present: policy, training, dataset, env
- ACT hyperparameters match spec: chunk_size=100, n_obs_steps=1, dim_model=512, n_heads=8, n_encoder_layers=4, n_decoder_layers=7
- dataset.repo_id='local/so101_teleop' matches params/data_pipeline.yaml
- dataset.fps=30 matches params/data_pipeline.yaml
- Training params match spec: lr=1e-5 (AdamW), batch_size=8, steps=100000, save_freq=25000

RL Config:
- Structural sections present: algorithm, environment, training, drive, domain_randomization, reward, network
- Drive gains match params/control.yaml: stiffness=40.0, damping=4.0, max_effort=5.0
- sim_dt=0.005 matches params/physics.yaml simulation.timestep
- decimation=10 matches params/control.yaml
- PPO hyperparameters match spec: lr=3e-4, gamma=0.99, lam=0.95, clip_range=0.2, entropy_coef=0.01, value_loss_coef=0.5
- Domain randomization matches spec: mass ±20%, friction ±30%, actuator_gain ±15%
- num_envs=1024 matches spec

No code changes required — verification-only subtask.

subtasks.description: CLI help verification — Run --help for all Python scripts to verify argparse is properly configured and all expected CLI arguments are present.

verification.command: python3 training/il/train_act.py --help && python3 training/rl/train_rl.py --help && python3 training/eval/evaluate_policy.py --help && echo 'ALL CLI HELP OK'

subtasks.notes:
CLI help verification passed for all 3 Python scripts:

1. training/il/train_act.py --help: ✅ All expected args present (--repo_id, --batch_size, --steps, --save_freq, --output_dir, --lr, --config, --data_pipeline_yaml)
2. training/rl/train_rl.py --help: ✅ All expected args present (--task, --num_envs, --headless, --max_iterations, --checkpoint_freq, --output_dir, --seed, --config, --control_yaml)
3. training/eval/evaluate_policy.py --help: ✅ All expected args present (--policy_type {il,rl} [required], --checkpoint_path [required], --task, --num_episodes, --max_steps, --headless, --success_threshold, --il_config, --rl_config)

All scripts:
- Have proper Korean help descriptions
- Follow argparse best practices (required args marked, default values documented)
- Support --config overrides pointing to YAML config files
- evaluate_policy.py correctly uses choices={il,rl} for --policy_type
- No code changes required — verification-only subtask

parallel_groups.reason: Both depend on nothing (no dependencies), modify different file sets (training/il/ vs training/rl/)

parallelism.speedup_estimate: 1.5x faster than sequential — phases 1 and 2 can run in parallel

acceptance_criteria: All 6 files created and syntactically valid
training/il/train_act.py can parse config and show --help
training/il/config.yaml has all ACT hyperparameters from spec (chunk_size=100, dim_model=512, etc.)
training/rl/so101_env.py defines ArticulationCfg + Reach task with SO101_CFG
training/rl/train_rl.py can parse config and show --help
training/rl/config.yaml has PPO hyperparameters and domain randomization settings
training/eval/evaluate_policy.py can show --help and supports both IL/RL via --policy_type
All scripts follow project patterns (import guards, logging, argparse, headless-first, Korean docstrings)

verification_steps.name: Python Syntax Validation
verification_steps.command: python3 -c "import ast; [ast.parse(open(f).read()) for f in ['training/il/train_act.py','training/rl/so101_env.py','training/rl/train_rl.py','training/eval/evaluate_policy.py']]; print('OK')"
verification_steps.expected_outcome: All Python files parse without syntax errors

verification_steps.name: YAML Validation
verification_steps.command: python3 -c "import yaml; [yaml.safe_load(open(f)) for f in ['training/il/config.yaml','training/rl/config.yaml']]; print('OK')"
verification_steps.expected_outcome: All YAML files load without errors

verification_steps.name: CLI Help Test
verification_steps.command: python3 training/il/train_act.py --help && python3 training/rl/train_rl.py --help && python3 training/eval/evaluate_policy.py --help
verification_steps.expected_outcome: All scripts show help without errors

verification_strategy.reasoning: Medium risk — new files following established patterns. Cannot run actual training (requires GPU + Isaac Sim runtime). Verification limited to syntax, config, and CLI validation. Full runtime verification requires manual testing in the soarm conda environment with Isaac Sim 5.1.0.

commands: python3 -c "import ast; [ast.parse(open(f).read()) for f in ['training/il/train_act.py','training/rl/so101_env.py','training/rl/train_rl.py','training/eval/evaluate_policy.py']]; print('OK')"
python3 -c "import yaml; [yaml.safe_load(open(f)) for f in ['training/il/config.yaml','training/rl/config.yaml']]; print('OK')"

e2e_tests.notes: E2E requires Isaac Sim 5.1.0 + GPU runtime. Must be tested manually: conda activate soarm && python training/il/train_act.py --steps 1000 && python training/rl/train_rl.py --task Isaac-SO101-Reach-v0 --num_envs 1024 --headless
